architecture.summary: CodePRM is a process reward model for code generation that scores individual reasoning steps by taking both the reasoning trace and code execution feedback as input. During training, thought steps are labeled with pass rates derived from their generated code and paired with execution feedback. At inference, a Generate-Verify-Refine (GVR) pipeline uses CodePRM as a process verifier to identify and correct errors in the thought process during code search, enabling dynamic refinement of reasoning paths.

biblio.title: CodePRM: Execution Feedback-enhanced Process Reward Model for Code Generation

claims.statement: CodePRM leverages code execution feedback to build a code PRM, training it to score individual thought steps by taking both the reasoning process and code execution feedback as input to distinguish between high-quality and low-quality thought steps.
A Generate-Verify-Refine (GVR) pipeline uses CodePRM as a process verifier to dynamically identify and correct errors in the thought process during code search at inference time.
CodePRM outperforms strong baselines, significantly enhancing code generation performance.
Process Reward Models for code generation require addressing the gap between thoughts and code, which differs from mathematical reasoning PRMs.
